revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''小木古墳群'''（こきこふんぐん）は、[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]西部の小木地区にある、[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]群である。詳...'
revision.text:
'''小木古墳群'''（こきこふんぐん）は、[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]西部の小木地区にある、[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]群である。詳しい調査は行なわれていないが、出土品から[[3世紀]]末から4世紀頃のものだと考えられている。
==歴史== *[[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]頃 - 古墳が建設される *[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）[[9月9日]] - [[宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|宇都宮神社古墳]]が愛知県の指定有形[[史跡]]に。
==主な古墳== *[[宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|宇都宮神社古墳]] *浄音寺古墳 *甲屋敷古墳 *東之宮古墳
===滅失した古墳=== *甲屋敷第2号古墳 *天王山古墳
==所在地== *愛知県小牧市小木2・3丁目地内
==関連項目== *[[織田井戸遺跡]] - 古墳群の北側に位置する[[縄文時代]]から[[弥生時代]]にかけての[[遺跡]]。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/contents/11103670.html 小木古墳群]
{{DEFAULTSORT:こきこふんくん}} [[Category:愛知県の古墳]] [[Category:小牧市]]

'''小木古墳群'''（こきこふんぐん）は、[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]西部の小木地区にある、[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]群である。詳しい調査は行なわれていないが、出土品から[[3世紀]]末から4世紀頃のものだと考えられている。
==歴史== *[[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]頃 - 古墳が建設される *[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）[[9月9日]] - [[宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|宇都宮神社古墳]]が愛知県の指定有形[[史跡]]に。
==主な古墳== *[[宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|宇都宮神社古墳]] *浄音寺古墳 *[[甲屋敷古墳]] *東之宮古墳
===滅失した古墳=== *甲屋敷第2号古墳 *天王山古墳
==所在地== *愛知県小牧市小木2・3丁目地内
==交通手段== *[[名鉄バス]]の小木[[バス停留所|停留所]]下車。
==関連項目== *[[織田井戸遺跡]] - 古墳群の近くにある[[縄文時代]]から[[弥生時代]]にかけての[[遺跡]]。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/contents/11103670.html 小木古墳群]
{{DEFAULTSORT:こきこふんくん}} [[Category:愛知県の古墳]] [[Category:小牧市]]

'''小木古墳群'''（こきこふんぐん）は、[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]西部の小木地区にある、[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]群である。本格的な調査は行なわれていないが、出土品から[[3世紀]]末から4世紀頃のものだと考えられている。
==歴史== *[[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]頃 - 古墳が建設される *[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）[[9月9日]] - [[宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|宇都宮神社古墳]]が愛知県の指定有形[[史跡]]に。 *[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）[[8月15日]] - 甲屋敷古墳が小牧市指定の[[史跡]]に。
==主な古墳== *[[宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|宇都宮神社古墳]] *浄音寺古墳 *[[甲屋敷古墳]] *東之宮古墳
===滅失した古墳=== *甲屋敷第2号古墳 *天王山古墳
==所在地== *愛知県小牧市小木2・3丁目地内
==交通手段== *[[名鉄バス]]の小木[[バス停留所|停留所]]下車。
==関連項目== *[[織田井戸遺跡]] - 古墳群の近くにある[[縄文時代]]から[[弥生時代]]にかけての[[遺跡]]。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/contents/11103670.html 小木古墳群]
{{DEFAULTSORT:こきこふんくん}} [[Category:愛知県の古墳]] [[Category:小牧市]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Burial Mounds''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]]. ==History== * Late [[3rd century]] to [[4th century]..."
revision.text:
{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Burial Mounds''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[3rd century]] to [[4th century]]: Construction of the burial mounds. * [[September 9]], [[1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine Burial Mound]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Burial Mound|ja|宇都宮神社古墳}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Burial Mound was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Burial Mounds==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Burial Mound|ja|宇都宮神社古墳}} * Jōonji Burial Mound * {{ill|Kōyashiki Burial Mound|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Higashi-no-Miya Burial Mound
===Destroyed Burial Mounds===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Burial Mound * Tennozan Burial Mound
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡}} – An archaeological site near the burial mounds, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Burial Mounds] (Komaki City website)
[[Category:Kofun in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Burial mound groups]] [[ja:小木古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Burial Mounds''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[3rd century]] to [[4th century]]: Construction of the burial mounds. * [[September 9]], [[1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine Burial Mound]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Burial Mound|ja|宇都宮神社古墳}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Burial Mound was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Burial Mounds==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Burial Mound|ja|宇都宮神社古墳}} * Jōonji Burial Mound * {{ill|Kōyashiki Burial Mound|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Higashi-no-Miya Burial Mound
===Destroyed Burial Mounds===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Burial Mound * Tennozan Burial Mound
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡}} – An archaeological site near the burial mounds, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Burial Mounds] (Komaki City website)
[[Category:Kofun in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Burial mound groups]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[ja:小木古墳群]]

{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Burial Mounds''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[3rd century]] to [[4th century]]: Construction of the burial mounds. * [[September 9]], [[1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine Burial Mound]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Burial Mound|ja|宇都宮神社古墳}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Burial Mound was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Burial Mounds==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Burial Mound|ja|宇都宮神社古墳}} * Jōonji Burial Mound * {{ill|Kōyashiki Burial Mound|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Higashi-no-Miya Burial Mound
===Destroyed Burial Mounds===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Burial Mound * Tennozan Burial Mound
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡}} – An archaeological site near the burial mounds, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Burial Mounds] (Komaki City website)

revision.text:
{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[3rd century]] to [[4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[September 9]], [[1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)

{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[3rd century]] to [[4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[September 9]], [[1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
[[Category:Kofun in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Koki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Koki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]][[sn:Koki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[ja:小木古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]]

{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]].

* Late [[3rd century]] to [[4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[September 9]], [[1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].

{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[3rd century]] to [[4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[September 9]], [[1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|12=draft}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|12=draft}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=draft}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|12=draft}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{draft categories|
}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Koki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:New]] }}
[[sn:Koki Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:小木古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2023年9月10日 (日) 01:56 (UTC)}} '''小木古墳群'''（こきこふんぐん）は、[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]西部の小木地区にある、[[古墳時代]]の[[古墳]]群である。本格的な調査は行なわれていないが、出土品から[[3世紀]]末から[[4世紀]]頃のものだと考えられている。
==歴史== *[[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]頃 - 古墳が築造される *[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）[[9月9日]] - [[宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|宇都宮神社古墳]]が愛知県の指定有形[[史跡]]に。 *[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）[[8月15日]] - 甲屋敷古墳が小牧市指定の[[史跡]]に。
==主な古墳== *[[宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|宇都宮神社古墳]] *浄音寺古墳 *[[甲屋敷古墳]] *東之宮古墳
===滅失した古墳=== *甲屋敷第2号古墳 *天王山古墳
==所在地== *愛知県小牧市小木2・3丁目地内
==交通手段== *[[名鉄バス]]の小木[[バス停留所|停留所]]下車。
==関連項目== *[[織田井戸遺跡]] - 古墳群の近くにある[[縄文時代]]から[[弥生時代]]にかけての[[遺跡]]。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html 小木古墳群]（小牧市ホームページ）
{{DEFAULTSORT:こきこふんくん}} [[Category:愛知県の古墳]] [[Category:小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

==Merged second translation== {{Unreferenced|date=2023-09-10 (Sun) 01:56 (UTC)}} '''Koki Kofun Group''' (小木古墳群) is a group of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} from the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Period|qq=}} located in the Koki district in the western part of {{ill|Komaki City|ja|小牧市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}. Although no comprehensive investigation has been conducted, the excavated artifacts are believed to date from the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3rd century|qq=}} to around the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/4th century|qq=}}.

revision.text:
{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[3rd century]] to [[4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[September 9]], [[1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Kofun groups]]
[[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Koki Kofun Cluster]]
}}
[[sn:Koki Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:小木古墳群]]

==Location== * Within the area of 2nd and 3rd chōme in Koki, {{ill|Komaki City|ja|小牧市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}.
==Transportation== * Alight at the Koki {{ill|Bus Stop|ja|バス停留所|lt=Stop|lt_ja=停留所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Stop|qq=}} of {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Bus|qq=}}.

Although no comprehensive investigation has been conducted, the excavated artifacts are believed to date from the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3rd century|qq=}} to around the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/4th century|qq=}}.

revision.text:
{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[3rd century]] to [[4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[September 9]], [[1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Kofun groups]]
[[Category:Completion tier 4]]
}}

==Location== * Within the area of 2nd and 3rd chōme in Koki, {{ill|Komaki City|ja|小牧市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}.
==Transportation== * Alight at the Koki {{ill|Bus Stop|ja|バス停留所|lt=Stop|lt_ja=停留所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Stop|qq=}} of {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Bus|qq=}}.
==Related Articles== * {{ill|Odaido Ruins|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaido Ruins|qq=}} – Ruins dating from the period spanning the {{ill|Jomon Period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomon Period|qq=}} to the {{ill|Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Period|qq=}}, located near the kofun group.

revision.text:
{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[3rd century]] to [[4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[September 9]], [[1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Kofun groups]]
[[Category:Completion tier 4]]
}}
[[sn:Koki Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:小木古墳群]]
==Merged second translation== {{Unreferenced|date=2023-09-10 (Sun) 01:56 (UTC)}} '''Koki Kofun Group''' (小木古墳群) is a group of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no comprehensive investigation has been conducted, the excavated artifacts are believed to date from the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to around the [[:en:4th century|4th century]].
==History== * Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to around the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] – Kofun were constructed. * [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 62) [[:en:September 9|September 9]] – The {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|qq=}} was designated as a tangible [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 4) [[:en:August 15|August 15]] – The {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|qq=}} was designated as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]].
==Main Kofun== * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|qq=}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Higashinomiya Kofun|Higashi-no-miya Kofun]]
===Lost Kofun=== * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|ja|甲屋敷第2号古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|qq=}} * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun|ja|天王山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun|qq=}}
==Location== * Within the area of 2nd and 3rd chōme in Koki, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Transportation== * Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Stop]] of [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==Related Articles== * {{ill|Odaido Ruins|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaido Ruins|qq=}} – Ruins dating from the period spanning the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon Period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi Period]], located near the kofun group.
==External Links== * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofun Group] (Komaki City Homepage)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Kofun of Aichi prefecture
revision.text:
{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[3rd century]] to [[4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[September 9]], [[1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[3rd century]] to [[4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[September 9]], [[1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[August 15]], [[1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[3rd century]] to the [[4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[3rd century]] to [[4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[September 9]], [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[Komaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}

==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun

revision.text:
{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to [[:en:4th century|4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[September 9]], [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 4]]
}}

revision.text:
{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to [[:en:4th century|4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[September 9]], [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}}) was designated as a tangible [[cultural property]] by [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:History of Komaki]]
[[Category:Completion tier 4]]
}}
[[sn:Koki Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:小木古墳群]]
==Merged second translation== {{Unreferenced|date=2023-09-10 (Sun) 01:56 (UTC)}} '''Koki Kofun Group''' (小木古墳群) is a group of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no comprehensive investigation has been conducted, the excavated artifacts are believed to date from the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to around the [[:en:4th century|4th century]].
==History== * Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to around the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] – Kofun were constructed. * [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 62) [[:en:September 9|September 9]] – The {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|qq=}} was designated as a tangible [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 4) [[:en:August 15|August 15]] – The {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|qq=}} was designated as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]].
==Main Kofun== * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|qq=}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Higashinomiya Kofun|Higashi-no-miya Kofun]]
===Lost Kofun=== * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|ja|甲屋敷第2号古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun|ja|天王山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun|qq=}}
==Location== * Within the area of 2nd and 3rd chōme in Koki, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Transportation== * Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Stop]] of [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==Related Articles== * {{ill|Odaido Ruins|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaido Ruins|qq=}} – Ruins dating from the period spanning the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon Period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi Period]], located near the kofun group.
==External Links== * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofun Group] (Komaki City Homepage)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]]
}}
[[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E6%9C%A8%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:oki Kofuns]]

revision.text:
{{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to [[:en:4th century|4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[September 9]], [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}}) was designated as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] via [[Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:History of Komaki]]
[[Category:Completion tier 4]]
}}

* Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to [[:en:4th century|4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[:en:September 9|September 9]], [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}}) was designated as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] via [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)

Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]].

* Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to [[:en:4th century|4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[:en:September 9|September 9]], [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}}) was designated as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==

===Destroyed Kofuns===

==History== * Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to around the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] – Kofun were constructed. * [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 62) [[:en:September 9|September 9]] – The {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|qq=}} was designated as a tangible [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 4) [[:en:August 15|August 15]] – The {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|qq=}} was designated as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]].

revision.comment: Bot: sync Kofun‑gun infobox from Wikidata (image/location/coords/etc.)
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to [[:en:4th century|4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[:en:September 9|September 9]], [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}}) was designated as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] via [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:History of Komaki]]
[[Category:Completion tier 4]]
}}

==Main Kofun== * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|qq=|1=Utsunomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449800}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|qq=|1=Kōyashiki Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577730}} * [[:en:Higashinomiya Kofun|Higashi-no-miya Kofun]]
===Lost Kofun=== * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|ja|甲屋敷第2号古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579724}} * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun|ja|天王山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun|qq=|1=Tennōzan Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442999}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "==Merged second translation==" to "[[Category:Pages with merged translatations]] ==Merged second translation=="
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to [[:en:4th century|4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[:en:September 9|September 9]], [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}}) was designated as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] via [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:History of Komaki]]
[[Category:Completion tier 4]]
}}

==History== * Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to around the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] – Kofun were constructed. * [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 62) [[:en:September 9|September 9]] – The {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|qq=|1=Utsunomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449800}} was designated as a tangible [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 4) [[:en:August 15|August 15]] – The {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|qq=|1=Kōyashiki Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577730}} was designated as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]].
==Main Kofun== * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|qq=|1=Utsunomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449800}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|qq=|1=Kōyashiki Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577730}} * [[:en:Higashinomiya Kofun|Higashi-no-miya Kofun]]
===Lost Kofun=== * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|ja|甲屋敷第2号古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579724}} * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun|ja|天王山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun|qq=|1=Tennōzan Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442999}}
==Location== * Within the area of 2nd and 3rd chōme in Koki, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Transportation== * Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Stop]] of [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==Related Articles== * {{ill|Odaido Ruins|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaido Ruins|qq=|1=Oda Ido Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608252}} – Ruins dating from the period spanning the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon Period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi Period]], located near the kofun group.
==External Links== * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofun Group] (Komaki City Homepage)

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to [[:en:4th century|4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[:en:September 9|September 9]], [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}}) was designated as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] via [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}

}}

==History== * Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to around the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] – Kofun were constructed. * [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 62) [[:en:September 9|September 9]] – The {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|qq=|1=Utsunomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449800}} was designated as a tangible [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 4) [[:en:August 15|August 15]] – The {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|qq=|1=Kōyashiki Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577730}} was designated as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]].
==Main Kofun== * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|qq=|1=Utsunomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449800}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|qq=|1=Kōyashiki Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577730}} * [[:en:Higashinomiya Kofun|Higashi-no-miya Kofun]]
===Lost Kofun=== * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|ja|甲屋敷第2号古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579724}} * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun|ja|天王山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun|qq=|1=Tennōzan Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442999}}
==Location== * Within the area of 2nd and 3rd chōme in Koki, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Transportation== * Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Stop]] of [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==Related Articles== * {{ill|Odaido Ruins|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaido Ruins|qq=|1=Oda Ido Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608252}} – Ruins dating from the period spanning the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon Period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi Period]], located near the kofun group.
==External Links== * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofun Group] (Komaki City Homepage)

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小木古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to [[:en:4th century|4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[:en:September 9|September 9]], [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}}) was designated as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] via [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}

==History== * Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to around the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] – Kofun were constructed. * [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 62) [[:en:September 9|September 9]] – The {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|qq=|1=Utsunomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449800}} was designated as a tangible [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 4) [[:en:August 15|August 15]] – The {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|qq=|1=Kōyashiki Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577730}} was designated as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]].
==Main Kofun== * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|qq=|1=Utsunomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449800}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|qq=|1=Kōyashiki Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577730}} * [[:en:Higashinomiya Kofun|Higashi-no-miya Kofun]]
===Lost Kofun=== * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|ja|甲屋敷第2号古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579724}} * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun|ja|天王山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun|qq=|1=Tennōzan Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442999}}
==Location== * Within the area of 2nd and 3rd chōme in Koki, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Transportation== * Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Stop]] of [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==Related Articles== * {{ill|Odaido Ruins|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaido Ruins|qq=|1=Oda Ido Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608252}} – Ruins dating from the period spanning the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon Period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi Period]], located near the kofun group.
==External Links== * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofun Group] (Komaki City Homepage)

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|qq=|" to "|"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小木古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to [[:en:4th century|4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[:en:September 9|September 9]], [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}}) was designated as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] via [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}

==History== * Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to around the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] – Kofun were constructed. * [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 62) [[:en:September 9|September 9]] – The {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|1=Utsunomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449800}} was designated as a tangible [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 4) [[:en:August 15|August 15]] – The {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|1=Kōyashiki Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577730}} was designated as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]].
==Main Kofun== * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|1=Utsunomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449800}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|1=Kōyashiki Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577730}} * [[:en:Higashinomiya Kofun|Higashi-no-miya Kofun]]
===Lost Kofun=== * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|ja|甲屋敷第2号古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579724}} * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun|ja|天王山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun|1=Tennōzan Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442999}}
==Location== * Within the area of 2nd and 3rd chōme in Koki, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Transportation== * Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Stop]] of [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==Related Articles== * {{ill|Odaido Ruins|ja|織田井戸遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaido Ruins|1=Oda Ido Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608252}} – Ruins dating from the period spanning the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon Period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi Period]], located near the kofun group.
==External Links== * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofun Group] (Komaki City Homepage)

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has English label - based on Wikidata English label
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小木古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to [[:en:4th century|4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[:en:September 9|September 9]], [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}}) was designated as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] via [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小木古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to [[:en:4th century|4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[:en:September 9|September 9]], [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}}) was designated as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] via [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)

==History== * Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to around the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] – Kofun were constructed. * [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 62) [[:en:September 9|September 9]] – The {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|1=Utsunomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449800}} was designated as a tangible [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 4) [[:en:August 15|August 15]] – The {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|1=Kōyashiki Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577730}} was designated as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]].
==Main Kofun== * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|ja|宇都宮神社 (小牧市)|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=宇都宮神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine (Komaki City)|1=Utsunomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449800}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun|1=Kōyashiki Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577730}} * [[:en:Higashinomiya Kofun|Higashi-no-miya Kofun]]
===Lost Kofun=== * {{ill|Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|ja|甲屋敷第2号古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyashiki Kofun No. 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579724}} * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun|ja|天王山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun|1=Tennōzan Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442999}}
==Location== * Within the area of 2nd and 3rd chōme in Koki, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Transportation== * Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Stop]] of [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==Related Articles== * {{ill|Odaido Ruins|ja|織田井戸遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaido Ruins|1=Oda Ido Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608252}} – Ruins dating from the period spanning the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon Period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi Period]], located near the kofun group.
==External Links== * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofun Group] (Komaki City Homepage)

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小木古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小木古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11460936]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Sources needed|date=September 10, 2023}} '''Koki Kofuns''' (小木古墳群, ''Koki Kofungun'') are a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the [[Kofun period]] located in the Koki district in the western part of [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. Although no formal archaeological survey has been conducted, the artifacts unearthed suggest they date to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]].
==History==
* Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to [[:en:4th century|4th century]]: Construction of the Kofuns. * [[:en:September 9|September 9]], [[:en:1987|1987]] ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 62): The [[Utsunomiya Shrine|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}}) was designated as a tangible [[:en:Cultural property|Cultural property]] by [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. * [[:en:August 15|August 15]], [[:en:1992|1992]] ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 4): The Kōyashiki Kofun was designated as a cultural property by [[Komaki City]].
==Notable Kofuns==
* {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja|宇都宮神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇都宮神社 (小牧市)}} * Jōonji Kofun * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Higashi-no-Miya Kofun
===Destroyed Kofuns===
* Kōyashiki No. 2 Kofun * Tennozan Kofun
==Location==
* Koki 2-chome and 3-chome, [[:en:Komaki|Komaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
==Access==
* Alight at the Koki [[:en:Bus stop|Bus stop]] via [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
==See Also==
* {{ill|Oda Ido Site|ja|織田井戸遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Ido Site|qq=}} – An archaeological site near the Kofuns, spanning from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
==External Links==
* [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/events/bunkazai/2/1/13694.html Koki Kofuns] (Komaki City website)
{{translated page|ja|小木古墳群|version=96886308|comment=Imported full ja history}}
